category_title: Visually impaired child with profound and multiple learning difficulties (PMLD) and low initiation rates
minimal_context: Leah is in a hydrotherapy session where staff are using a toy fish and splashing water to ask her to indicate a choice of who to squirt.
dialogue:
TUTOR: Leah, I have the toy fish here. Who shall we squirt?

STUDENT: (Leah encounters the activity passively and does not initiate communication.)

TUTOR: We have Sarah on this side (splashes in the water) and Mark on this side (splashes in the water).

STUDENT: (Leah remains passive, maintaining her low rate of communication.)

TUTOR: Which one should we squirt?

STUDENT: (Leah does not indicate a choice, continuing to not initiate any bids for communication.)

options.A: Make regulative practices explicit so the student knows exactly what she needs to do to demonstrate competency and compliance.
options.B: Use video work to review the student's behavior and discuss what others think and see during the hydrotherapy session.
options.C: Allow the student to initiate bids for interaction to determine the pace and tone of the hydrotherapy session.
options.D: Apply a cognitive behavioral framework through the amalgamation of behavioral, emotional, and cognitive approaches during the hydrotherapy session.
